recalls.Description: Trane and American Standard have issued a recall for five models of Gas/Electric Packaged Units due to a potential gas leak that could result in a fire hazard. The affected models can be identified by their model and serial number ranges located on the back of the unit, with the brand name printed on the front. Consumers are urged to check their units against the specified model and serial number ranges to determine if they are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Trane and American Standard Gas/Electric Packaged Units Recalled Due to Gas Leak and Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Trane or American Standard at 800-889-0129, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET. More information can also be found online at https://www.trane.com/residential/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/ or by visiting www.trane.com/residential and selecting 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the page. For American Standard, visit https://www.americanstandardair.com/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/ and click on 'Product Recall' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled American Standard Gas/Electric Packaged Unit
Recalled Trane Two-Stage Gas/Electric Packaged Unit

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of gas leaking from the recalled two-stage gas furnace/air conditioners. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Trane U.S. Inc., of Davidson, North Carolina

Retailers.Name: HVAC distributors and dealers nationwide from January 2024 through March 2025 for between $8,250 and $12,900.

Hazards.Name: Unexpected fuel gas valve opening leading to gas leaks and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heating mode of the recalled units and contact the installation dealer for a free inspection and repair, which includes replacing the ignition board. Trane users can find their nearest dealer at www.trane.com/residential/en/dealer-locator/. American Standard users can locate their dealer at www.americanstandardair.com/find-your-dealer/. Direct support is also available from Trane or American Standard.

seo.metaDescription: Trane and American Standard recall Gas/Electric Packaged Units due to gas leak and fire hazards. Check your unit's model and serial numbers.

recalls.Description: Midea is recalling approximately 1.7 million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners, which were sold under various brand names including Midea, Comfort Aire, Danby, Frigidaire, Insignia, Keystone, LBG Products, Mr. Cool, Perfect Aire, and Sea Breeze. These air conditioners are designed for window installation and are available in three cooling capacities: 8,000, 10,000, and 12,000 BTU. The units are white, measuring about 22 inches wide and 14 inches high, with the brand name displayed on them. Model numbers can be found on a label located on the front right side of the unit. Each unit comes with a remote control and can be operated via a mobile app.
recalls.Title: Midea Recalls 1.7 Million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners Due to Mold Exposure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Midea at 888-345-0256 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email midea4028@midea.com. More information is available online at www.MideaUrecall.expertinquiry.com or www.midea.com/us under "RECALL INFORMATION."

Products.Name: U and U+ Window Air Conditioners
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.7 million (In addition, about 45,900 were sold in Canada)

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of at least 152 reports of mold in the air conditioners, including 17 reports of consumers experiencing symptoms such as respiratory infections, allergic reactions, coughing, sneezing and/or sore throats from mold exposure.

Manufacturers.Name: GD Midea Air-Conditioning Equipment Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Costco, Menards, Home Depot, Best Buy and other stores nationwide and online at Midea.com, Amazon.com, Costco.com, Menards.com, HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, Walmart.com, BJs.com, BestBuy.com and other websites from March 2020 through May 2025 for between $280 and $500.

Importers.Name: Midea America Corp., of Parsippany, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of mold growth due to pooled water not draining quickly, potentially causing respiratory issues or infections.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Midea for a free repair or a full or prorated refund based on the purchase date. For refunds, units must be returned using a free shipping label or by providing a photo of the cut power cord.

seo.metaDescription: Midea recalls 1.7 million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners due to mold exposure risk. Contact for repairs or refunds.
seo.ogTitle: Midea Recalls 1.7 Million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners Due to Mold Exposure Risk

recalls.Description: Daikin Comfort Technologies is recalling packaged air conditioning and heat pump units under the Amana, Daikin, and Goodman brands due to a potential fire hazard. These units, available in gray or green, can be identified by their model number located on the electrical connection side of the unit. To determine if your unit is affected by this recall, please visit www.goodmanmfg.com/package-recall.
recalls.Title: Daikin Comfort Technologies Recalls Air Conditioning and Heat Pump Units Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DCT at 833-324-5460 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.goodmanmfg.com/package-recall for further details.

Products.Name: Amana Air Conditioning and Heat Pump Units

Images.Caption: Recalled Goodman branded air conditioning and heat pump
Recalled air conditioning and heat pump model number label location

Manufacturers.Name: Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing L.P. (f/k/a Goodman Manufacturing Company L.P.), of Houston, Texas

Retailers.Name: Heating and cooling equipment independent dealers nationwide from January 2024 through March 2024 for between $4,500 to $7,500.

Hazards.Name: Incorrect electrical ratings on the unit's serial plate may lead to improper wiring, creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should refrain from opening the outside electrical panel and should immediately reach out to Daikin Comfort Technologies for a complimentary repair and inspection. DCT will directly contact all known purchasers.

seo.metaDescription: Daikin recalls Amana, Daikin, and Goodman air conditioning and heat pump units due to fire hazards. Check if your unit is affected.
